title: China's role as a pressure valve in sanctioned crude market
section_path: China's role as a pressure valve in sanctioned crude market
section_path_text: China's role as a pressure valve in sanctioned crude market
summary:
Main
China released a new batch of 2026 crude import quotas - what does it mean for the sanctioned crude market? By Emma Li

China's seaborne crude imports breach 12mbd for the first time
China's seaborne crude imports climbed to 12mbd in December 2025, marking a new record despite a not-fully-recovered domestic economy. More than 35mb (or ~1.1 mbd) of these arrivals flowed into onshore crude inventories, leaving adjusted refinery intake broadly flat month on month.

children.summary: China's seaborne crude imports climbed to 12mbd in December 2025, marking a new record despite a not-fully-recovered domestic economy. More than 35mb (or ~1.1 mbd) of these arrivals flowed into onshore crude inventories, leaving adjusted refinery intake broadly flat month on month.

children.title: Elastic demand meets structural constraints in China
section_path: Elastic demand meets structural constraints in China
children.section_path_text: Elastic demand meets structural constraints in China
children.summary:
China has emerged as the key destination for sanctioned crude in recent years, absorbing around 55% of global seaborne sanctioned exports since 2023. Image reference: Στιγμιότυπο οθόνης 2026-01-15 184333.png | assets/2026-01-15_chinas-role-as-a-pressure-valve-in-sanctioned-crude-market_img_cea3cf84ceb9ceb3cebcceb9cf8ccf84cf85_a799841333e0.png
Its flexible feedstock slate and ample onshore storage capacity allow the country to absorb large volumes of deeply discounted barrels.

children.title: Supplier outlook and substitution risks
children.summary: Despite being sanctioned, Yulong has been allocated 14mt of crude import quotas and is likely to receive additional volumes to better align with its 20 mtpa nameplate capacity. The refinery has now fully pivoted to Russian crude to sustain full runs, injecting at least ~250kbd of incremental Russian seaborne demand into Shandong since November 2025.
